title: Intercell moiré exciton complexes in electron lattices
type1_summary: No figure-ready source-data tables or directly replot-ready cleaned datasets were identified in the inspected files. The archive contents are dominated by DFT inputs/outputs and pseudopotentials.
type2_summary: The inspected files are primarily computational materials-science files: VASP structure files, PSML pseudopotentials, FDF input decks, and simulation outputs. These are raw/unprocessed or script/configuration files rather than cleaned source data.
classification_notes: The archive appears to contain only computational materials files associated with the paper's first-principles calculations, not the experimental source-data figures. Several filenames are duplicated in the inspection report; duplicates were classified consistently. The top-level ZIP itself is not a dataset type.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper includes supplementary source data and computational outputs, but the archive itself is just a container for multiple files.
file_classifications.file_evidence: ZIP archive containing mixed computational input/output files and pseudopotential files; not directly plottable data.
file_classifications.reasoning: This is a packaging container rather than a dataset table or a raw measurement file. It must be unpacked to assess the contents.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper reports first-principles calculations and structural relaxations for moiré heterobilayers.
file_classifications.file_evidence: VASP POSCAR structure file; atomic coordinates and lattice geometry for a WS2/WSe2 heterostructure with vacuum. Not figure-ready tabular data.
file_classifications.reasoning: This is a computational structure input/output file used for DFT modeling, not cleaned source data. It requires simulation context and cannot be directly plotted as a figure dataset.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Lattice vectors; atomic species; fractional/cartesian coordinates

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies WS2/WSe2 moiré heterobilayers and shows that the stacking geometry (R vs H) qualitatively changes the spatial structure of interlayer moiré excitons. Using photoluminescence, polarization-resolved PL, differential reflectance, AFM/PFM, and first-principles calculations, the authors identify a new intercell moiré exciton complex in H-stacked devices, where excitons bind to correlated charge order in neighboring moiré cells and produce stacking-dependent PL energy shifts and valley polarization.

figures.description: Schematic of an R-stacked WS2/WSe2 moiré supercell showing high-symmetry stacking positions A, B, C.
Schematic of an H-stacked WS2/WSe2 moiré supercell with the same high-symmetry positions.
Calculated spatial Kohn-Sham wavefunctions for band-edge electron and hole states in the R-stacked heterobilayer.
Calculated spatial Kohn-Sham wavefunctions for band-edge electron and hole states in the H-stacked heterobilayer, showing lateral misalignment and spread over three sites.
Optical microscope image of a near-60-degree twisted WS2/WSe2 heterobilayer device.
AFM image with inset PFM image showing the moiré pattern/triangular lattice.
PL spectrum of an interlayer moiré exciton in an R-stacked device at charge neutrality.
Gate-dependent PL intensity map versus moiré filling factor for the R-stacked device.
Extracted PL peak energies versus filling factor for the R-stacked device, with dashed lines indicating all measured gate voltages.
Gate-dependent PL intensity map versus moiré filling factor for the H-stacked device.
Extracted PL peak energies versus filling factor for the H-stacked device.
Cartoon comparing exciton-charge interaction in R-stacked versus H-stacked heterobilayers.
Schematic of intercell moiré exciton complex at v = -1/7 in H-stacked heterobilayer.
Schematic of intercell moiré exciton complex at v = -1/3 in H-stacked heterobilayer.
Schematic of intercell moiré exciton complex at v = -1 in H-stacked heterobilayer.
Circular-polarization-resolved PL for the R-stacked device under σ+ excitation.
Degree of circular polarization versus filling factor for the R-stacked device.
Circular-polarization-resolved PL for the H-stacked device under σ+ excitation.
Degree of circular polarization versus filling factor for the H-stacked device.
Schematic explaining doping-dependent PL polarization in R-stacked heterobilayers.
Schematic explaining doping-dependent PL polarization in H-stacked heterobilayers.
Calculated normal strain distribution maps for relaxed R- and H-stacked heterobilayers.
Calculated structural energy distribution for local stacking configurations in R- and H-stacked heterobilayers.
PFM images and gate-dependent PL/reflectance data used to assign moiré filling factors for Devices R1 and H1.
Zoomed PL maps and linecuts for Device R1 at fractional and integer fillings.
AFM/PFM and gate-dependent PL for additional R-stacked Device R2.
Polarization-resolved PL and extracted peak energies for additional R-stacked Device R3.
Zoomed PL spectra and linecuts for Device H1 at fractional and integer fillings.
Polarization-resolved PL and extracted peak energies for additional H-stacked Device H2.
Calculated Coulomb interaction energy between exciton and electron lattice versus filling factor for R- and H-stacked cases.
Polarization-resolved PL for Device R1 at 8 T magnetic field.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw experimental measurements include optical microscope images, AFM images, PFM images, PL spectra, polarization-resolved PL spectra, differential reflectance spectra, and SHG-based crystal orientation measurements. The paper also reports first-principles electronic-structure calculations and structural relaxations, which are computational outputs rather than experimental raw measurements.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Processed plots include gate-dependent PL intensity maps, extracted PL peak energies versus filling factor, degree of circular polarization versus filling factor, zoomed linecuts from spectral maps, and calculated strain/energy/wavefunction maps from DFT. Filling factors are assigned from gate voltages using a parallel-plate capacitance model and then adjusted using optical reflectance/PL features.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that the datasets generated during and/or analyzed during the study are available from the corresponding author upon reasonable request.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: No file formats, repository links, or raw-data archive are described. The paper only states that datasets are available from the corresponding author upon request.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: The paper presents processed spectral maps, extracted peak energies, polarization degrees, and calculated theory curves in the figures and extended data. It also describes filling-factor assignment from gate voltages and optical reflectance/PL features.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific source data are not explicitly mentioned as provided. The paper does not state that source data files accompany each figure.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper does not provide explicit source-data files, raw data archives, or scripts in the text. Only figure-level plots and a request-based data availability statement are given, so direct replotting from the paper text alone is not possible.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mix of experimental imaging/spectroscopy and computational outputs. The most important derived quantities are moiré filling factor, PL peak energy, and circular polarization degree, all extracted from gate-dependent optical spectra. Extended Data figures indicate multiple devices (R1-R3, H1-H2) and repeated measurements across cool-downs/spots, but the organization of underlying files is not specified.
